Video.summary: “The Century Race”: Dale believes he found the bike that will help him beat a rival in an annual race of 100 year old motorcycles. But will it run?
Video.title: The Century Race

Video.summary: A California collector gambles on a big payday from his Crocker/Caddy combo.
Video.title: Doubling Down

Video.summary: For Dale and Matt sometimes the dotted line to treasure splits and heads in opposite directions. In this edition of What’s in the Barn, time is thrust to the forefront of this multi-state, long distance, north, south search. Timeless and priceless 110 year old bikes, antique cars, airplanes, and the infamous Egberts 1930 neon Harley-Davidson sign that coined the phrase “Ride a Winner” are high on this father and son’s list of “must haves”. The clock ticks, both literally and figuratively, on one rare time piece and two old-timer’s cars, bikes, and automobiles.
Video.title: Time is Not On Their Side

Image.alt: Time is Not On Their Side
Time is Not On Their Side
Time is Not On Their Side

Video.summary: Promoters of the prestigious Amelia Island Concours have called on Dale to develop a show and field bikes for an “Orphan Bike” show. They have defined orphan bike as extremely rare, that aren't a Harley nor Indian machines.
Video.title: Orphan Bikes

Video.summary: “A Motorcycle Crime Story”: The horrific tale of a double murder over money and motorcycles.
Video.title: Motorcycle Crime Story

Video.summary: “Yes, Dale there is a 100 Year Old Harley”: Dale hopes his labor of love becomes a prized possession when a six-figure Harley goes to auction.
Video.title: 100 Year Old Harley

Video.summary: A thousand bulging barns of choppers, bobbers, and board trackers are emptied and on display in the Quad Cities.
Video.title: Davenport Swap

Video.summary: A vague tip leads Matt and Dale into a WWII era military warehouse and beyond.
Video.title: Peashooter

Video.summary: A cross-country race to lay claim to some of the world’s most sought after works specials.
Video.title: Hidden Hillclimbers

Video.summary: “12hrs. & Running”: Dale turns a surprise 60th birthday party into a challenge to his guests to start 120 of the rarest motorcycles in the world.
Video.title: 12 Hours and Running

Video.summary: Rickey, Bryan and Adam explore all the cars that should never have been built at Concourse d'lemons.
Video.title: Concourse d'lemons: One Man's Trash Is a Car Enthusiast's Treasure

Image.alt: Concourse d'lemons: One Man's Trash Is a Car Enthusiast's Treasure
Concourse d'lemons: One Man's Trash Is a Car Enthusiast's Treasure
Concourse d'lemons: One Man's Trash Is a Car Enthusiast's Treasure

Video.summary: One of a kind cars at Amelia Island Caffeine & Octane.
Video.title: Super Cars at Course d'Elegance

Video.summary: Caffeine & Octane at Ameilia Island have everything from classic to extraterrestrial.
Video.title: A New Generation Takes over Amelia Island Caffeine & Octane

Image.alt: A New Generation Takes over Amelia Island Caffeine & Octane
A New Generation Takes over Amelia Island Caffeine & Octane
A New Generation Takes over Amelia Island Caffeine & Octane

Video.summary: A new season kicks off with some big numbers: Featuring the world's fastest C8 Corvette, an overpowered Cortina Mark I, and a Buick Regal with over 1,700 horsepower
Video.title: Voices: Blackberry Smoke

Video.summary: C & O: North America's most diverse car show: Featuring custom Bugs, John Kaase fire breathing '56 Fords, and Chevy Silverados touching 1000HP
Video.title: Voices: Bishop Rob Wright

Video.summary: C & O Jacksonville: Featuring Hurley Haywood's Brumos Porsches, a street legal Indy car, and a dynamic '60 Olds
Video.title: Brumos Collection with Hurley

Video.summary: Jacksonville's C & O's colorful and quirky car culture.
Video.title: Graffiti Artist KBMER

Video.summary: C & O: North America's greatest car show
Video.title: Voices: Sport's Radio Legend, Mike Bell

Image.alt: Voices: Sport's Radio Legend, Mike Bell
Voices: Sport's Radio Legend, Mike Bell
Voices: Sport's Radio Legend, Mike Bell

Video.summary: Caffeine & Octane: North America's greatest car show
Video.title: Voices: Killer Mike

Video.summary: Amelia Island's own Caffeine & Octane car show,: Featuring $2,000 Wrek Racing Truck, David Patterson's favorite C&O cars, a plastic Citroen Mehari, and much more.
Video.title: Fuller's Fab 5

Video.summary: The Concours d'Elegance from Amelia Island: Featuring bomb-proof Conquest Knight XV, '69 Camaro Hagerty Fixer-Upper, and car-enthusiast John Oates.
Video.title: Generation Amelia

Video.summary: State-of-the-art engineering and passion projects take center stage on this edition of Caffeine & Octane. The revolutionary Delta Wing Racer turns on hot lap after hot lap at Road Atlanta, and driver Katherine Legge offers insight on how “less is more” when it comes to power to weight ratios; the dark world of a graffiti artist paints a picture of a car called Vengeance while the hosts share the eclectic hybrids of machines that make it to Caffeine and Octane with Vengance’s creator Totem.
Video.title: Delta Wing Racer with a Vengeance

Image.alt: Delta Wing Racer with a Vengeance
Delta Wing Racer with a Vengeance
Delta Wing Racer with a Vengeance

Video.summary: The word is out that Caffeine & Octane is the ultimate scene and the place to be seen. Even after 12 hours of racing at the Petit Le Mans endurance race, Henry Ford III, the great grandson of Henry Ford the industrial magnate and current member of Ford’s Board of Directors, showed up to take in the sights, address the crowds, and sign a one of a kind Ford Shelby GT350R. The GT350R will hit the road with Operation Hope to encourage young people to study math and science through motorsports; Covert car counterfeiter, Chuck Beck looks for Ford’s approval on his own version of a muscled up Festiva. YouTuber ‘That Dude in Blue’ David Patterson joins Skip and Bryan to get a special look at the motor-based philanthropic outreach and amazing cars that come with it done by John Hope Bryant, friend of Caffeine and Octane.
Video.title: Henry Ford III & Counterfeit Cars

Video.summary: Rickey and Bryan race outside of their comfort zones, each competing to find the raddest car collection. Rickey hangs out with classic racer, Ron Green, who shows him the ropes in the ultra light '83 Ralt RT5. Bryan visits a collection of 120 racing motorcycle owned by mechanic and racer Mike McConville. Mike takes Bryan motorcross racing in his backyard after showing off his high horsepowered car collection. Caffeine and Octane alongside Ron Green visit Atlanta's first Concours d'elegance to look at classic cars that have been perfectly maintained for decades.
Video.title: Go Fast Collections

Video.summary: Wayne Phears’ Jaguar E-Type was nothing more than a diamond in the rough before the mineral rights attorney took on the basket case. Caffeine & Octane brings viewers the story of how Phears’, through relentless research and determination, restored and reunited the #10 Jag that Enzo Ferrari called “the most beautiful car ever made”; Atlanta Wraps shows how wrapping Corvettes and Lamborghinis can color a car fast. Caffeine and Exotics is featured again to show off the coolest wraps Atlanta can supply.
Video.title: The Wrap on the Most Beautiful Cars

Image.alt: The Wrap on the Most Beautiful Cars
The Wrap on the Most Beautiful Cars
The Wrap on the Most Beautiful Cars

Video.summary: Nick Rutter walks you through step-by-step on how to remove stubborn bug splatter from your vehicle along with tips and techniques for using a waterless wash as well as a clay bar.
Video.title: Family Truckster Restoration Part 1

Video.summary: Nick Rutter provides tips and techniques for restoring dull, faded paint using a forced-rotation dual action polisher along with a graphene 3-in-1 ceramic polish, along with severe scratch and scuff removal.
Video.title: Family Truckster Restoration Part 2

Video.summary: When 4 Wheel Parts ask Truck U to build a signature series Jeep, we said yes. We’ll add a long arm suspension kit with 37 inch tires and take the new JK off-road.
Video.title: Signature Series Jeep

Video.summary: Desert Race Teams need support when they’re in the middle of nowhere. We’ll help one of those teams, by turning a stock Raptor SVT into the Ultimate Chase Truck.
Video.title: Ultimate Chase Truck

Video.summary: When it takes almost 900 horsepower to pull a pro sled truck down a track, things are going to break. Truck U steps in, to take out broken axles and upgrade them for good.
Video.title: Diesel Puller

Video.summary: Matt and Bruno bring in a 1982 GMC that has a lot of show and even more go. They’ll do some custom modifications to the Cummins engine and dress up the exterior.
Video.title: Show and Go

Video.summary: When you buy a new Dodge Ram Truck there are certain things you’ll want to do to get it ready to tow your toys.
Video.title: Dodge Ram Tow Truck

Video.summary: Like trucks, UTVs can be customized beyond our imaginations. But there are also some common problems that need to be fixed.
Video.title: UTV Fix

Video.summary: They guys diagnose and a repair a crank, no start problem on a Ford Powerstroke.
Video.title: Diesel No Start

Video.summary: When you start with a frame from a C-10 truck there is only one way for this project to go….down. We’ll give the C-10 frame a 2-inch drop and bolt on a ton of cool new parts.
Video.title: C-10 Frame Off Resto

Video.summary: From break up to make up, Jeff Gordon and Ray reunite to go on a quest to find their Chevrolet Monte Carlo that claimed victory in their final race together at Phoenix, AZ in 1999. John Bickford, Jeff Gordon’s stepdad, makes the call that reunites the old team. Jeff and Ray look to bum parts off their boss, Rick Hendrick, to make their vision a reality: get the car, replace the parts as they were, make it run and get it to auction.
Video.title: BREAK UP TO MAKEUP

Video.summary: One of the greatest battles in NASCAR history was Hall of Famer Richie Evans and rival speedster Jerry Cook. Ray is determined to uncover and revive the cars that made these men famous.Ray purchases one of Richie’s original #61 modifieds from Jimmy Spencer and restores it to its original glory while simultaneously building a replica of Jerry Cook’s #38. Throughout the build process, Ray uncovers stories about these legends through the eyes of guys who were in Rome, NY to witness it firsthand, like Robin Pemberton and Steve Hmiel.Both cars will hit the track at Martinsville, site of one of the greatest races in modified history and the tragic death of Richie Evans himself. Legends live again, as Ray recreates one of the racing world’s most celebrated rivalries on a track that has seen it all.
Video.title: BATTLE FOR ROME

Video.summary: General Motors VP of global design Ed Welburn takes AmeriCarna behind the scenes to talk about the history and development of the company’s concept cars that never made it to production. In a rare tour of the factory’s inner design studio, Ray gets an exclusive look behind the creative curtain at the GM plant in Detroit.
Video.title: SECRETS OF GM

Video.summary: In this episode, Ray celebrates the 50th birthday of the Ford Mustang through the lens of three distinct Mustang owners, who each hold a slice of the car’s incredible history. In 1964, Chicago schoolteacher Gail Wise unknowingly purchased the first ever Mustang 3 days before the car became available to the general public. Her husband reveals the incredible restoration that brought the earliest Mustang ever sold back to its 60’s glory. (Ford Motor Company knows she has the car and has verified it was sold before it was legal to sell.) John Grafelman is a farmer in Illinois and life-long Ford lover. A few years ago he found an old car hidden in a barn. It took some time, but he eventually convinced his friend to sell it to him. After some detective work, he discovers he purchased 1968 Ford Design Chief Larry Shinoda’s personal car that he tinkered with to invent the BOSS 302.Lastly, Ray travels to Dayton Ohio to interview Gale Holderman, the Ford stylist who won the company’s contest to draw and design the Mustang.
Video.title: FIRST MUSTANG

Video.summary: Post-World War II, a large generation of American soldiers was looking for cars that mirrored the incredible sports cars they saw overseas in Europe. After searching for an affordable way to recreate these cars, one manufacturer responded with a durable, cost effective option: a line of fiberglass sports cars. Very few of these cars still exist but there are collectors who are constantly looking to save parts and pieces of these virtually unknown vehicles. We learn about these rare cars from Geoff Hacker and his team at Forgotten Fiberglass, and take a visit to the Smithsonian Museum, which houses a fiberglass car of its own. This episode presents a unique look at how the narrative of American wartime experiences often shaped the nation’s automotive demands.
Video.title: FORGOTTEN FIBERGLASS

Video.summary: Ray explores buying a unique, hand built car the Schultz family has kept in their New Jersey home for years. The car was originally called the Schultz El Fierro but it’s officially titled in New Jersey as a “Schultz Special” from 1958. It was used to drag race and held numerous racing records, but most important to the family was that it was driven by the legendary Carroll Shelby. With a frame and style much similar to the Shelby Roadster, the Schultz family wonders if it inspired one of the most well known automobiles in America. In another epic New Jersey story, Ray finds Mario Andretti’s 1965 IndyCar. The catch: it’s completely in pieces hidden away in a garage. The iconic driver talks about his days on the track with that car and Ray attempts to negotiate a deal to buy the parts.
Video.title: DISCOVERIES IN JERSEY

Video.summary: How do you cast a car in a movie? What are the secrets to the build? Hollywood car designers give Ray the ultimate tour of some memorable movie cars, including a look at the recently found “Hells Chariot”, the last surviving car from Grease. We also hear the backstory to “Bumble Bee” from Transformers, take a look at the making of the tanks for Hunger Games and stumble upon the iconic 1958 Chevy Impala from American Graffiti.
Video.title: MOVIE CARS

Video.summary: While visiting the estate of automotive genius and land speed record holder Willie Glass, Ray uncovers a set of extremely rare Ardun cylinder heads, which is a hot rodders dream discovery. He also visits the infamous H & H Flatheads outside of Los Angeles and discovers a surprise connection to the Earnhardt family.
Video.title: HOT ROD HOLY GRAIL

Video.summary: A massive sinkhole opened underneath the National Corvette Museum in Kentucky. Eight amazing cars were swallowed up, including the 1,000,000th Corvette. AmeriCarna was given exclusive access to document the removal of the cars from the sinkhole and the painstaking restoration of the 1,000,000th Corvette.
Video.title: CORVETTE SINK HOLE

Video.summary: On the beaches of Wildwood, NJ, an iconic vintage race, The Race of Gentleman, takes place annually: period cars, period dress, pure authenticity. Ray puts a car in the race with a very special driver, who turns out not to be a Gentleman.
Video.title: ROAR AT THE SHORE

Video.summary: Scott Borchetta, CEO and Founder of Big Machine Label Group, and his brother, Mark, love racing just as much as anyone. From Indy to Nashville, Ray finds he has a lot in common with the Borchetta brothers. This celebrity-filled episode features musicians Tim McGraw, Cassadee Pope and IndyCar driver Josef Newgarden.
Video.title: CARS AND MUSIC STARS

Video.summary: C & O Jacksonville: Featuring Hurley Haywood's Brumos Porsches, a street legal Indy car, and a dynamic '60 Olds
Video.title: Episode 1
